summary: sensivity ships a Windows persistence backdoor disguised as 'OneDriveUpdate' with an obfuscated native game-cheat payload
details: On import or first invocation, launcher.js silently establishes Windows logon persistence by writing an HKCU Run-key entry named 'OneDriveUpdate' (impersonating Microsoft OneDrive) that points wscript.exe at a sibling OneDrive.Standalone.Updater.vbs, which in turn relaunches launcher.js hidden at every login. The same launcher.js detaches itself as a supervisor process (writing.sensivity-supervisor.pid), sets process.title to 'Runtime Broker' to mimic a Windows system process, spawns with windowsHide:true and stdio:'ignore', and monkey-patches console.log/warn/error to strip 'sens.node' and the install path from any output. At module load it eval()s an 81KB heavily obfuscated server.obf.js (obfuscator.io-style rotated string array, RC4-decoded literals, hex identifiers) which loads sens.node — a 6.8MB closed-source Windows x64 binary whose strings ('LeftFoot','LeftHand','Head','Aimbot','Triggerbot','Teleport') and the package's public/index.html UI ('Start Cheat','Aimbot','Triggerbot','ESP','Teleport') identify it as a game-cheat tool reading/writing other processes' memory. A worker writes a temp PowerShell script every 3 seconds (-ExecutionPolicy Bypass) that enumerates all chrome.exe MainWindowTitle values. Any require() of this package, even briefly during testing, leaves a perpetual hidden background process running under a spoofed name with logon persistence under a Microsoft-impersonating Run key.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
